pave the way.explanation_en: to make it easier for something to happen or for someone to do something
pave the way.example_sentence: His research paved the way for a new medical treatment.
pave the way.original_sentence: Believing a solution paves the way to solution.

eliminate.explanation_en: to remove or get rid of something completely
eliminate.example_sentence: We need to eliminate all errors before submitting the report.
eliminate.original_sentence: Eliminate impossible, won't work, can't do, no use trying from your thinking and speaking vocabularies.

paralyze.explanation_en: to make someone unable to think or act normally
paralyze.example_sentence: Fear of failure can paralyze your decision-making.
paralyze.original_sentence: Don't let tradition paralyze your mind.

receptive.explanation_en: willing to consider or accept new ideas and suggestions
receptive.example_sentence: The manager was receptive to my suggestions for the project.

experimental.explanation_en: based on new ideas or methods that have not been tested before
experimental.example_sentence: The chef is known for his experimental cooking style.

progressive.explanation_en: favoring or implementing new ideas or modern methods
progressive.example_sentence: The company has a progressive policy regarding remote work.
progressive.original_sentence: Be progressive in everything you do.

self-improvement.explanation_en: the process of making yourself a better or more knowledgeable person
self-improvement.example_sentence: Reading books is a great way to focus on self-improvement.
self-improvement.original_sentence: There is no limit to self-improvement.

sound.explanation_en: sensible, reliable, or based on good judgment
sound.example_sentence: She gave me some very sound advice.
sound.original_sentence: When you ask yourself, how can I do better, sound answers will appear.

capacity.explanation_en: the ability to do or produce something
capacity.example_sentence: He has a great capacity for learning new languages.
capacity.original_sentence: Capacity is a state of mind.

shortcut.explanation_en: a quicker or more direct way to do something
shortcut.example_sentence: There are no shortcuts to becoming an expert; it takes practice.
shortcut.original_sentence: Asking yourself this question puts your mind to work to find intelligent shortcuts.

output.explanation_en: the amount of something produced by a person, machine, or factory
output.example_sentence: The factory increased its output by 20% this year.
output.original_sentence: The success combination in business is, do what you do better, improve the quality of your output, and do more of what you do, increase the quantity of your output.

obtain.explanation_en: to get something, especially by effort
obtain.example_sentence: You need to obtain permission before entering the building.
obtain.original_sentence: Ask and listen, and you'll obtain raw material for reaching sound decisions.

raw material.explanation_en: basic information or facts that can be used to create something else
raw material.example_sentence: These interviews provided the raw material for his new book.
raw material.original_sentence: Ask and listen, and you'll obtain raw material for reaching sound decisions.

monopolize.explanation_en: to take control of something so that others cannot use it
monopolize.example_sentence: Don't monopolize the conversation; let others speak too.
monopolize.original_sentence: Remember, big people monopolize the listening.

stretch.explanation_en: to push someone to use their full abilities or potential
stretch.example_sentence: This project will really stretch your creative skills.

stimulated.explanation_en: feeling excited or interested in something
stimulated.example_sentence: I felt very stimulated after the interesting lecture.

associate with.example_sentence: It is important to associate with positive people.
associate with.original_sentence: Associate with people who can help you think of new ideas, new ways of doing things.

mix with.explanation_en: to meet and talk to other people
mix with.example_sentence: At the party, I had the chance to mix with people from different backgrounds.
mix with.original_sentence: Mix with people of different occupational and social interests.
